# Story 1.6: Admin Session Revocation Endpoint

Status: done

<!-- Generated by BMad create-story workflow on 2026-05-10. Ultimate context engine analysis completed - comprehensive developer guide created. -->

## Story

As an admin,  
I want gọi `POST /api/v1/auth/sessions/:userId/revoke` để force logout user khác,  
so that tôi có thể thu hồi quyền truy cập của thu ngân nghỉ việc hoặc thiết bị mất.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Admin revoke thành công:** Given admin đã đăng nhập bằng Bearer token hợp lệ và role admin, when `POST /api/v1/auth/sessions/:userId/revoke`, then server verify caller qua `JwtAuthGuard` + `@Roles('admin')` + `RolesGuard`, set `users.is_revoked = true` cho target user, set `revokedAt = now()` cho mọi row `refresh_tokens` của target user chưa revoked, và trả `204 No Content` không body.
2. **Forbidden cho non-admin/anonymous:** Given caller là cashier hoặc không có Bearer token hợp lệ, when gọi revoke endpoint, then response là RFC 7807 `application/problem+json`; cashier nhận `403 type=https://pos.example/errors/forbidden`, anonymous/invalid token nhận `401 type=https://pos.example/errors/unauthorized`.
3. **Không leak cross-tenant / user không tồn tại:** Given `userId` không tồn tại hoặc thuộc tenant/store khác caller, when admin gọi revoke, then response `404 application/problem+json` `type=https://pos.example/errors/not-found`; detail không tiết lộ user có tồn tại ở tenant khác hay không.
4. **Refresh sau revoke:** Given target user có refresh token cookie hợp lệ và access token JWT 7 ngày trong IndexedDB, when target user gọi `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh`, then existing `AuthService.refresh()` thấy `users.isRevoked=true` hoặc token đã revoked và trả `401 type=https://pos.example/errors/session-revoked`; auth cookies được clear theo behavior Story 1.5.
5. **Idempotent edge:** Given target user đã `isRevoked=true` hoặc refresh tokens đã revoked, when admin revoke lại cùng target trong cùng tenant/store, then vẫn trả `204`, không throw conflict, không tạo token mới, không đổi user khác.
6. **Layering + security:** Controller không gọi Prisma trực tiếp; business logic nằm ở `UsersService`; Prisma queries nằm ở users repository/service layer đúng pattern; không log token/password/hash; mọi lỗi đi qua `ProblemDetailsFilter`.
7. **Tests/gates:** Có unit tests cho `users.service.ts` và controller/guard behavior; e2e cover positive admin revoke, negative cashier 403, anonymous 401, cross-tenant/not-found 404, revoke đã revoked 204, và refresh sau revoke trả `session-revoked`. Gates tối thiểu pass: `cd pos-api && npx prisma validate && npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test && npm run test:e2e && npm run build`.

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Tạo Users module cho admin session revocation (AC: 1, 3, 5, 6)
  - [x] Tạo `pos-api/src/users/users.module.ts` và import vào `AppModule`; không phá `AuthModule`, `HealthModule`, `PrismaModule`, `ThrottlerModule` hiện có.
  - [x] Tạo `pos-api/src/users/users.service.ts` với method kiểu `revokeUserSession(targetUserId: string, context: TenantContext): Promise<void>`.
  - [x] Nếu cần tách query, tạo `pos-api/src/users/repositories/users.repository.ts`; tránh để Prisma query trong controller.
  - [x] Dùng Prisma `user.updateMany` scoped bằng `id + tenantId + storeId`, sau đó kiểm tra `count`; `count=0` thì throw `NotFoundException` hoặc typed problem tương đương để filter map thành `type=.../not-found`.
  - [x] Revoke tokens bằng `refreshToken.updateMany({ where: { userId: targetUserId, revokedAt: null }, data: { revokedAt: now } })` sau khi xác nhận target cùng tenant/store; `RefreshToken` không có tenant/store nên bắt buộc không update tokens trước khi target user scoped pass.
- [x] Expose endpoint đúng auth/RBAC contract (AC: 1, 2, 6)
  - [x] Update `pos-api/src/auth/auth.controller.ts` thêm `@Post('sessions/:userId/revoke')`, `@HttpCode(204)`, `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard)`, `@Roles('admin')`.
  - [x] Inject `UsersService` vào AuthController hoặc tạo controller trong auth module path tương đương; path cuối cùng phải là `/api/v1/auth/sessions/:userId/revoke` vì `main.ts` đã set global prefix `api/v1`.
  - [x] Lấy `tenantId/storeId/userId/role` từ `@TenantContext()` hoặc `req.tenantContext`; thiếu context dưới authenticated route phải fail an toàn, không update global.
  - [x] Không thêm CSRF requirement cho revoke endpoint; đây là Bearer-protected admin API, không cookie-auth endpoint.
- [x] Sửa/wire role guard nếu cần để endpoint thực sự được bảo vệ (AC: 1, 2)
  - [x] Kiểm tra `JwtAuthGuard` đang sign payload role là Prisma enum `Admin`/`Cashier`; `RolesGuard.normalizeRole()` hiện lower-case thành `admin`/`cashier`, vì vậy `@Roles('admin')` phải hoạt động.
  - [x] Đảm bảo `RolesGuard` được provider ở module dùng endpoint. Nếu dùng `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard)` trong `AuthController`, add `RolesGuard` vào providers/imports phù hợp; không chỉ rely vào class tồn tại trong `common/guards`.
  - [x] Preserve existing `/auth/login`, `/auth/refresh`, `/auth/logout` behavior và tests.
- [x] Preserve refresh/session behavior từ Story 1.5 (AC: 4, 6)
  - [x] Không tạo bảng token mới, không lưu raw refresh token, không đổi JWT TTL 7 ngày.
  - [x] Reuse existing `AuthService.refresh()` behavior: user revoked hoặc matching token revoked trả `session-revoked`, không `session-expired`.
  - [x] Không cố invalidate offline access token ngay lập tức; architecture chấp nhận target vẫn dùng local/offline cho tới `exp` hoặc lần online tiếp theo.
- [x] Add tests (AC: 2, 3, 4, 5, 7)
  - [x] Unit-test `UsersService`: success updates scoped user + active tokens; `count=0` throws not found; already revoked returns success; no token rows still success.
  - [x] Unit/integration-test guard wiring if practical: cashier role denied with `ForbiddenException`/403.
  - [x] Extend `pos-api/test/auth.e2e-spec.ts` or create `session-revocation.e2e-spec.ts` with fixtures for admin/cashier/cross-tenant user.
  - [x] E2E assert `204` body empty for admin revoke; `403` cashier problem type; `401` anonymous/invalid bearer; `404` for non-existent/cross-tenant target; post-revoke `/auth/refresh` returns `session-revoked` and clear cookies.
  - [x] Keep throttling/rate-limit tests from Story 1.5 stable; if endpoint tests share app instance/IP, avoid accidentally exhausting `/auth/login` or `/auth/refresh` limits.
- [x] Update docs and run gates (AC: 7)
  - [x] Update `pos-api/README.md` with admin revoke endpoint example and warning that offline access token remains usable locally until JWT exp by architecture trade-off.
  - [x] Run required gates and record exact commands/results in Dev Agent Record.

## Dev Notes

### Scope boundaries

- Story này chỉ implement backend admin session revocation endpoint `POST /api/v1/auth/sessions/:userId/revoke`. Không implement FE admin UI, FE token storage/interceptor, role-routing pages, or broader user CRUD. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 1.6`]
- Endpoint path belongs under auth API boundary per architecture table, but business operation may live in `users.service.ts` because architecture tree marks `users.service.ts # admin revocation FR5`. Acceptable implementation: AuthController route delegates to UsersService. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md` lines around API boundary and BE tree]
- Preserve Story 1.5 auth contract exactly: login/refresh/logout endpoints, refresh CSRF double-submit, cookie names, JWT payload, RFC7807 problem types, and throttling. [Source: `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/1-5-auth-backend-endpoints-login-refresh-logout.md`]

### Current codebase state to modify/preserve

- Existing `AuthController` has `POST auth/login`, `auth/refresh`, `auth/logout`; global prefix in `main.ts` makes public URLs `/api/v1/auth/...`. Add route prefix relative to controller; do not hardcode `/api/v1`. [Source: `pos-api/src/auth/auth.controller.ts`; `pos-api/src/main.ts`]
- Existing `AuthService.refresh()` already compares against all refresh token candidates, distinguishes `revokedAt` → `session-revoked`, checks `user.isRevoked`, rotates token transactionally, and clears cookies on 401 in controller. Do not regress this. [Source: `pos-api/src/auth/auth.service.ts`; Story 1.5 review patches]
- Existing `RefreshTokenRepository` has `findUserById`, `findRefreshCandidates`, `revoke`, `rotate`; it does **not** currently expose `revokeAllByUser`. Add a focused method there only if you intentionally keep refresh-token updates in auth repository; otherwise a Users repository can update `refreshToken` after scoped user verification. [Source: `pos-api/src/auth/repositories/refresh-token.repository.ts`]
- `User` and `RefreshToken` are not in `SCOPED_MODELS` of tenant `$extends`; Prisma will not auto-scope them. Every user lookup/update for this story must manually include caller `tenantId` and `storeId` to avoid cross-tenant revocation. [Source: `pos-api/src/prisma/tenant-scope.extension.ts`; `pos-api/prisma/schema.prisma`]
- `RolesGuard` exists but is not globally registered; if route uses it, ensure DI provider availability. `RolesGuard` normalizes `Admin`/`Cashier` to lower-case, so use `@Roles('admin')`, not `@Roles('Admin')`. [Source: `pos-api/src/common/guards/roles.guard.ts`; `roles.guard.spec.ts`]
- `ProblemDetailsFilter` maps standard `NotFoundException` → `https://pos.example/errors/not-found`, `ForbiddenException` → `.../forbidden`, and `UnauthorizedException` → `.../unauthorized`. Prefer standard Nest exceptions unless custom title/detail is required. [Source: `pos-api/src/common/filters/problem-details.filter.ts`; `problem-types.ts`]

### Architecture compliance and security guardrails

- Follow backend layering: Controller → Service → Repository → PrismaService. Controller can parse route params/context and call service only; service owns revocation transaction/business rules; repository/Prisma layer owns DB queries. [Source: `_bmad-output/project-context.md#Cấu-trúc-Module`]
- Use a single `now = new Date()` per revoke request so all rows get consistent `revokedAt`; do not use raw SQL. [Source: project rule: PrismaService via repositories, raw query forbidden unless scoped]
- Use `updateMany` for idempotent token revoke. Re-revoking an already revoked target must still return 204; do not treat `refreshToken.updateMany.count=0` as failure if user exists in scope. [Source: Story 1.6 AC edge case]
- Cross-tenant hiding is mandatory: target user must be selected/updated by `id + tenantId + storeId`. Returning 404 for both absent and other-tenant users prevents tenant enumeration. [Source: Story 1.6 AC]
- Access-token revocation is intentionally not immediate for offline local usage. Do not add a token blacklist, websocket kick, shorter JWT TTL, or online polling in this story. [Source: architecture risk/trade-off section]
- If admin revokes themselves, epics do not forbid it. Recommended behavior: allow it idempotently because endpoint targets `userId`; document if choosing to block self-revoke. Do not silently special-case without tests.

### API contract details

```http
POST /api/v1/auth/sessions/:userId/revoke
Authorization: Bearer <admin access token>
```

- `:userId` is UUID string matching `users.id`. No request body.
- Success: `204 No Content`; no JSON envelope.
- Cashier caller: `403 application/problem+json`, `type=https://pos.example/errors/forbidden`.
- Missing/invalid bearer: `401 application/problem+json`, `type=https://pos.example/errors/unauthorized`.
- Missing/cross-tenant target: `404 application/problem+json`, `type=https://pos.example/errors/not-found`.
- After success: target user's refresh rows have `revoked_at` set and `users.is_revoked=true`; subsequent refresh returns `401 type=https://pos.example/errors/session-revoked`.

### Expected file structure changes

```text
pos-api/
├── src/
│   ├── app.module.ts                         # import UsersModule if created
│   ├── auth/
│   │   ├── auth.controller.ts                # add POST sessions/:userId/revoke
│   │   └── auth.module.ts                    # import UsersModule or provide UsersService/RolesGuard
│   ├── users/
│   │   ├── users.module.ts                   # NEW
│   │   ├── users.service.ts                  # NEW: revoke target sessions
│   │   ├── users.service.spec.ts             # NEW
│   │   └── repositories/users.repository.ts  # optional NEW if keeping Prisma out of service
│   └── common/guards/roles.guard.ts          # use as-is unless tests reveal provider/wiring issue
test/
└── auth.e2e-spec.ts or session-revocation.e2e-spec.ts
```

Do not edit `pos-web/` in this story.

### Previous story intelligence

- Story 1.5 completed auth endpoints and patched two review issues: revoked refresh tokens now return `session-revoked`, and e2e covers revoked session + rate-limit. This story should reuse that behavior instead of reimplementing refresh classification. [Source: `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/1-5-auth-backend-endpoints-login-refresh-logout.md#Review-Findings`]
- Story 1.5 file list shows auth tests and README already exist; extend them rather than creating duplicate auth docs/test setup. [Source: previous story File List]
- Recent commits: `b465543 feat(pos-api): add auth backend endpoints`, `140cde2 feat(pos-api): add demo seed data`, `c050672 feat(pos-api): add tenant scoped infrastructure`, `3c58e23 feat(pos-api): add prisma foundation schema`. Pattern: small backend feature commits with tests/gates; keep this story similarly scoped.

### Testing and verification requirements

- Minimum gates before review:
  - `cd pos-api && npx prisma validate`
  - `cd pos-api && npm run typecheck`
  - `cd pos-api && npm run lint`
  - `cd pos-api && npm test`
  - `cd pos-api && npm run test:e2e`
  - `cd pos-api && npm run build`
- For e2e fixture setup, create users/tokens directly with Prisma using UUID v7 and bcrypt cost 12, following existing `auth.e2e-spec.ts` patterns. Keep tenant/store relationships valid.
- To test refresh after revoke: login as target cashier first to capture `refresh_token` and `csrf_token`, login/admin token separately, admin revoke target, then call `/auth/refresh` with target cookies + `X-CSRF-Token`; assert 401 `session-revoked`.
- Assert 204 response has no body; do not return `{ success: true }`.
- If local Postgres unavailable for e2e, mark blocker explicitly instead of claiming pass.

### Open questions / caveats for developer

- Architecture tree mentions `users.service.ts`, while API boundary lists module `auth` for the endpoint. Recommended split: route in `AuthController`, business in `UsersService`, `UsersModule` imported by `AuthModule` or `AppModule` as needed.
- `User` table has `isRevoked` but no inverse “unrevoke” endpoint in scope. Once revoked, target cannot login/refresh unless future admin/user-management story resets the flag. This matches FR5; do not add unrevoke here.

### Review Findings

- [x] [Review][Patch] E2E auth tests are order-dependent and fail in the full gate because the shared login throttle bucket is exhausted before the post-revoke refresh scenario; isolate login/refresh rate-limit state per test (e.g. unique tracker/IP/header for normal auth calls or reset throttler storage) so `npm run test:e2e` is deterministic. [pos-api/test/auth.e2e-spec.ts:411]

### Completion Notes List

- Added `POST /api/v1/auth/sessions/:userId/revoke` under AuthController with `JwtAuthGuard`, `RolesGuard`, and `@Roles('admin')`; no CSRF requirement added.
- Added Users module/service/repository path; scoped user update uses `id + tenantId + storeId` before revoking active refresh tokens, preserving cross-tenant 404 behavior.
- Revoke is idempotent for already-revoked users and users with no active refresh tokens.
- Existing Story 1.5 refresh behavior is reused: post-revoke refresh returns `session-revoked` and clears auth cookies.
- Documented endpoint and offline JWT-expiry trade-off in README.
- Patched review finding by clearing `ThrottlerStorageService` state in `beforeEach()` for auth e2e tests, preventing the rate-limit spec from exhausting login/refresh buckets needed by later scenarios.
- Made helper-created e2e user emails unique per call to avoid collisions when tests/data persist across repeated full gate runs.
